explanation: The natural language requirement says that whenever V_Mot enters the range [1, 12], then, starting after at most 1 time units, Spd_Act must be in the range [100, 1000]. To capture this, we detect the rising edge of the condition 'V_Mot in [1,12]' and specify that in the next time step, 'Spd_Act in [100,1000]' must hold. The predicate 'V_Mot in [1,12]' is written as (V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12), and the target condition is (Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). Template 21 fits this case, ensuring that on a rising edge of the precondition, in the next step the consequence holds.

The requirement states that whenever V_Mot enters the range [1, 12], then in response, after at most 1 time unit, Spd_Act must be within [100, 1000]. To formalize "V_Mot enters the range [1, 12]", we detect a rising edge of (V_Mot >= 1) & (V_Mot <= 12), i.e., it is false and becomes true. The response "Spd_Act in [100, 1000]" is expressed as (Spd_Act >= 100) & (Spd_Act <= 1000). We want this response to occur in the next time step (after at most 1 time unit). Template 20 expresses this: after a rising edge in the trigger condition, the response must be true in the next time unit, and if the trigger is true already at the current step, the response must already hold. This aligns fully with the intent of the English statement.

The property requires that whenever V_Mot enters the range [1, 12] (i.e., it was not in that range, but now is), then within 1 time unit, Spd_Act must enter and stay in the range [100, 1000]. The template 20 captures the semantics of a response within 1 time unit after entering the triggering condition. The triggering condition is that V_Mot is at least 1 and at most 12. The response required is that Spd_Act is at least 100 and at most 1000. The boolean expressions combine the relevant atomic propositions to represent these ranges.

The property specifies that whenever V_Mot enters the range [1, 12], which can be expressed as (V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12), then in response, starting after at most 1 time unit, Spd_Act must be in the range [100, 1000], or (Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). The appropriate template for such a pattern is: 'Globally, if triggering condition holds, then in the next step the response condition holds', which matches template index 11: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)). Setting bool_exp2 to the conjunction for V_Mot in range, and bool_exp3 to the conjunction for Spd Act in range, the property ensures that the response occurs immediately after the trigger, which is within 1 time unit as required.

This LTL property states that whenever V_Mot enters the range [1,12] (i.e., V_Mot becomes greater than or equal to 1 and less than or equal to 12), then in the next time unit, the value of Spd_Act should be between 100 and 1000 (inclusive). The chosen template (11) G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)) encodes this requirement by triggering on the event that V_Mot moves into the specified range and ensuring the response occurs after at most one time unit.

The LTL property states that globally (G), when V_Mot enters the range [1, 12] (i.e., V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12 becomes true), then in response after at most 1 time unit, Spd_Act must be at least 100 and at most 1000 (Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). The template chosen enforces that whenever the trigger condition just became true, the response must be satisfied in the next time step, which matches the 'starting after at most 1 time units' requirement.

The property states that whenever V_Mot is within the range [1, 12] (i.e., V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 AND V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12), then in response, within 1 time unit, Spd_Act must be within [100, 1000] (i.e., Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 AND Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). This is best expressed using the template G (cond -> (X (Spd_Act in range))) because 'starting after at most 1 time units' means that in the next time unit after the condition holds, the requirement must be satisfied.

The natural language requirement specifies that whenever V_Mot enters the specified range [1, 12], then within at most 1 time units after this entry, Spd_Act must be in the corresponding range [100, 1000]. The template that best fits this is one that, upon the condition (V_Mot enters [1, 12]), forces (within 1 time unit) the invariant for Spd_Act to fall within [100, 1000]. The entry of the range is captured by (¬InRange & X InRange), and the response is specified to occur in the next time step. Here, InRange for V_Mot is (V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12) and the range for Spd_Act is (Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). Therefore, we use template 20: ((G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X bool_exp3))) & (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)), with: bool_exp2 = V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12, bool_exp3 = Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000, N_DURATION is not applicable (null).

The input specifies that whenever 'V_Mot' enters the range [1, 12] (i.e., becomes greater than or equal to 1 and less than or equal to 12), then within at most 1 time unit afterward, 'Spd_Act' must be between 100 and 1000 (inclusive). Entry into the range means the condition was previously not true and now becomes true. The template 21 is best suited: it encodes that on such a rising edge, after at most 1 time unit, the property must hold: i.e., whenever ((! (old condition)) & (next condition)) is true, then (X (X desired consequence)). We use '(V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12)' as the entered range condition and '(Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000)' as 'Spd_Act' in required range. N_DURATION is not used by this pattern but set for completeness. The formula ensures that on each 'entering' event, the desired action must follow after at most 1 step.

The natural language specification requires that whenever V_Mot enters the interval [1, 12], then in response, within at most 1 time unit afterward, Spd_Act must be in the range [100, 1000]. The condition that V_Mot enters the range is best captured as (not in range at previous instant) and (in range at current instant), which translates to ((! (V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12)) & (X (V_Mot_greaterThanOrEqual_1 & V_Mot_lessThanOrEqual_12))). The response is that at the next time instant, Spd_Act is in [100, 1000], i.e., (Spd_Act_greaterThanOrEqual_100 & Spd_Act_lessThanOrEqual_1000). The template that captures this 'entry event triggers response after at most 1 time unit' is 20: ((G (((! bool_exp2) & (X bool_exp2)) -> (X bool_exp3))) & (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 is 'V_Mot in range' and bool_exp3 is 'Spd_Act in range'. N_DURATION is not used in this template, so null.
